purpose: Grammar likelihood-ratio AV signal (LambdaG, arXiv:2403.08462): build a count-based n-gram language model over POS-tag sequences and score a query document's grammar log-likelihood under a REFERENCE-AUTHOR POS-LM versus a BACKGROUND POS-LM. Reports a signed log-LR (lambda_g, length-normalized lambda_g_per_token = logL_ref - logL_bg) plus a per-sentence and per-n-gram decomposition. The likelihood-ratio sibling of voice_distance's Burrows Delta (same voice_coherence surface, same no-verdict posture). M1 is stdlib: the n-gram LM is pure collections/math; the spaCy POS parse (shared variance_audit._NLP) is the only model-gated step, so without it the surface ABSTAINS (available:false / missing_dependency). NO VERDICT: the headline is a continuous signed real with both ratio halves exposed; the only categorical is a 3-level PROVISIONAL leaning band (background_leaning / indeterminate / author_leaning); no is_ai / same_author / verdict key, and the surface NEVER ranks authors (verification, one reference author — not attribution). Anti-Goodhart: reference/background drawn from one manifest must be held-out DISJOINT by id (self-scoring refused). The claim license refuses the same-author AND the AI/human inferences; thresholds operator-side / PROVISIONAL.
use_when: You want a generative grammar likelihood-ratio of a query against a reference-author corpus vs a background (the LR companion to voice_distance's Burrows Delta)
You have a reference-author corpus AND a held-out-disjoint background corpus, both English, with a parser installed
You want an interpretable, white-box, stdlib signal with a per-n-gram explanation, not a neural AV score
do_not_use_when: No spaCy / en_core_web_sm available (the surface abstains)
You want a same-author/different-author verdict or an AI/human provenance call (the surface refuses both)
The reference and background overlap, are thin, or are register-mismatched (the LR is corpus-relative and inflates or flips), or the text is non-English

inputs.reference: reference-author corpus (--reference-dir or --manifest + a persona/split/register filter)
inputs.background: background corpus (--background-dir or --manifest + a persona/split/register filter)

compute.cost_note: stdlib n-gram LM over POS sequences; POS tags come from the shared spaCy parse (CPU). No model trained or downloaded.

examples.description: Grammar log-LR of a query against a reference-author dir vs a background dir
Reference and background drawn from one manifest by persona (held-out disjoint by id)

references: Grammar as a Behavioral Biometric: Using Cognitively Motivated Grammar Models for Authorship Verification (LambdaG, arXiv:2403.08462): https://arxiv.org/abs/2403.08462
